Ruling:
Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Parties appeared on 5/22/07. Defendant Tumsco, Inc.'s Motion For Protective Order Limiting Plaintiffs' Discovery To Tumsco Product At Issue is granted in part and limited in part. MOTION TO QUASH SUBPOENAS
Off Calendar Jan-23-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearance:
Cheryl L. White, Esq., for Donald W. Dye, Plaintiff/Moving Party
Linda M. Moroney, Esq., for 3M Company, Defendant/Opposing Party
Mark Jackson, Esq., for Tumsco, Inc., Defendant/Opposing Party
B. Thomas French, Esq., for Vallen Corporation, Defendant/Opposing Party
Matthew S. Cole, Es., for Moltex Metric, Inc., Defendant/Opposing Party
Ruling:
Tentative Ruling adopted with modification: Plaintiffs Donald Dye, Valarie Dye's Motion For Order Limiting and/or Quashing 3M Company's Deposition Subpoenas For Production Of Documents; alternatively, Motion For Protective Order is granted as it seeks a protective order limiting the medical subpoenas to pulmonary and/or cardiac conditions related to Plaintiff's silicosis. Plaintiff's waiver of his medical privacy is limited and does not extend to all communications with any physician at any time. The broad invasion of privacy sought by these subpoenas does not reflect the required specificity and careful balancing of interests.
As to the HealthNet subpoena
The employment records subpoena is limited to Plaintiff's exposure to silica dust and his use of masks and cab filters during the course of his employment.
The court finds that the overbreadth and the opposition to the motion were without substantial justification. Sanctions are awarded against Defendant in the amount of $1,790, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order.

Modification: Sanctions are awarded against Defense counsel in the amount of $1,790. to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Stay Of Proceedings In This Case
Off Calendar Dec-13-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Cheryl L. White of Brent Coon & Associates, 489-7420, appearing for plaintiffs and responding parties
D. Mark Jackson of Bassi Martini Edlin & Blum, 397-9006, appearing for defendant Tumsco, Inc.
Mary Kirk Hillyard of Prindle Decker & Amaro, LLP, 909-341-3761, appearing for defendant Moldex Metric, Inc.
Linda Moin Moroney of Gordon & Rees, LLP, 986-5900, appearing for defendant 3M Company
B. Thomas French of Hassard Bonnington, LLP, 288-9800, appearing for defendant Vallen Corporation

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT 3M COMPANY'S Motion For Stay Of Proceedings In This Case is denied. No case has held that dismissal of one co-defendant automatically stays trial as to all other defendants even where there is joint and several liability. Possibility that juries might decide case against remaining defendants differently than case against differently situated appealing defendants is not irreconcilable result for purposes of section 916. Prevailing party to prepare form of order. (301/PJB/JF) Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR #8189
